title: High-frequency spinal cord stimulation at 10 kHz for widespread pain: outcomes from combined cervical and thoracic placements
study_question: What are long-term outcomes of HF10-SCS for widespread neuropathic or nociplastic pain?
population_assessed: 38 implanted patients with widespread upper and lower body neuropathic or nociplastic pain, including fibromyalgia
outcome_measures: NRS pain, RMDQ disability, employment, opioid use, satisfaction, adverse events
inclusion_criteria: Widespread upper and lower body neuropathic or nociplastic pain; combined cervical and thoracic HF10-SCS trial
answer_to_study_question: HF10-SCS showed sustained pain relief, improved disability and employment, reduced opioid use, and high satisfaction.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields showed near-complete agreement. Participant count was 38 by all models. Study design favored uncontrolled case series over controlled cohort because no comparator group appears in any extraction. Follow-up used Anthropic and OpenRouter consensus; outcome measures merged overlapping items.
field_level_decisions_json: {"study_design": "Overrode two low-specificity cohort labels; article reads as retrospective survey/case series without control group.", "title": "Used agreed title wording, shortened slightly to stay under word limit.", "population_assessed": "Used implanted cohort size 38, not initial 45 candidates.", "follow_up_duration": "Accepted 2.3 ± 1.7 years from two models; Gemini had zero confidence.", "outcome_measures": "Merged common endpoints across models."}
